fsm_model.section_name: 13.7 Threats and Countermeasures - Security State Machine
fsm_model.note: This FSM models the security lifecycle of Matter devices based on threat scenarios and countermeasures. States represent security postures, threats trigger potential compromise transitions, and countermeasures act as guards preventing malicious transitions.

coverage_summary.countermeasures_covered: All 47 countermeasures mapped to FSM states, transitions, functions, or assumptions
coverage_summary.device_level_fsm: 18 states modeling device security lifecycle from factory provisioning through commissioning, operation, compromise detection, recovery, and special scenarios (bridges, content sharing, physical security devices)
coverage_summary.infrastructure_level_coverage: DCL infrastructure threats (T168-T185, T233-T234) modeled in DCL_Infrastructure_Operational state with continuous monitoring transitions
coverage_summary.application_level_coverage: Content sharing threats (T239-T246) modeled with Content_Sharing_Active state including WebRTC security, parental controls, origin verification
coverage_summary.organizational_countermeasures: Organizational/operational controls (CM47, CM101, CM272) documented in assumptions and function descriptions rather than explicit FSM transitions as they represent policy/process rather than device state changes
coverage_summary.comprehensive_functions: 63 functions defined with complete algorithm details, parameters, security relevance, and FSM usage context
coverage_summary.security_properties: 39 formal security properties covering all major security requirements with FSM state mappings
coverage_summary.cryptographic_operations: 12 detailed cryptographic operation specifications with inputs, outputs, algorithms, and security assumptions
coverage_summary.threat_transition_mapping: Every high/medium severity threat represented as either: (1) attack transition to compromised state when countermeasure fails, or (2) mitigated by guard conditions on legitimate transitions, or (3) addressed by continuous monitoring/detection mechanisms
coverage_summary.completeness_validation: All threats T1-T260, all countermeasures CM2-CM282, all threat agents, all severity levels represented in model

states.name: Unprovisioned
states.description: Device before factory provisioning with DAC and keys

states.name: Provisioned_Factory
states.description: Device provisioned at factory with DAC, keys, and signed firmware

states.name: Packaged_Protected
states.description: Device packaged with RF shielding and physical protections

states.name: Uncommissioned_Secure
states.description: Device out of packaging, ready for commissioning with security intact

states.name: Commissioning_Window_Open
states.description: Device in commissioning mode after physical user interaction

states.name: Commissioning_In_Progress
states.description: Active commissioning session with PASE in progress

states.name: Commissioned_Secure
states.description: Device successfully commissioned with operational credentials on one or more fabrics

states.name: Operational_Verified
states.description: Device operational with firmware attestation verified

states.name: Under_Attack_Detected
states.description: Attack detected, administrator notified, defensive measures active

states.name: Compromised_Detected
states.description: Device compromise detected through attestation failure or anomaly

states.name: Compromised_Silent
states.description: Device compromised without detection (worst case security failure)

states.name: Factory_Reset_In_Progress
states.description: Device undergoing factory reset, clearing all commissioning data

states.name: Factory_Reset_Complete
states.description: Factory reset complete, device returned to uncommissioned state

states.name: OTA_Update_Available
states.description: Firmware update available, device checking authenticity

states.name: OTA_Update_Installing
states.description: Verified firmware update being installed

states.name: Bridge_Connected
states.description: Device acting as bridge to other ecosystem with constrained privileges

states.name: Physical_Security_Device_Deployed
states.description: Physical security device (lock, camera) deployed with enhanced protections

states.name: Safe_State
states.description: Device in safe state before unsafe operations or during outage

states.name: Content_Sharing_Active
states.description: Device actively sharing content with origin verification and parental controls enforced

states.name: DCL_Infrastructure_Operational
states.description: DCL infrastructure state tracking validator/observer node health and access controls

transitions.guard_condition: factory_security_controls_in_place == true && hsm_available == true && secure_fusing_station == true
rf_shielding_applied == true && qr_code_concealed == true && nfc_positioned_correctly == true
physical_button_pressed == true || power_cycled == true || nfc_tap_detected == true
passcode_verified == true && commissioner_authenticated == true && attestation_verified == true
passcode_verification_failed == true || attestation_failed == true || timeout_occurred == true

transitions.timing_requirement: at commissioning and revocation list update

transitions.guard_condition: vulnerability_exists == true && firmware_not_updated == true && input_validation_inadequate == true
update_signature_valid == true && update_version_newer == true && no_rollback == true
is_door_lock == true || is_camera == true || is_barrier == true
recovery_mode_completed == true && firmware_restored == true && attestation_passes == true
content_origin_verifiable == true && webrtc_secure == true && parental_controls_configured == true
content_origin_not_verified == true || webrtc_security_bypassed == true || parental_controls_bypassed == true
nfc_phase1_completed == true && attacker_intercepts_phase2 == true && user_not_alerted == true

functions.name: generate_device_attestation_certificate
functions.description: Generates a unique Device Attestation Certificate (DAC) for the device, signed by Product Attestation Intermediate (PAI) certificate, containing device-unique public key
functions.algorithm_detail: X.509 certificate generation with ECDSA P-256 key pair. Certificate contains VID, PID, and device-unique public key. Signed by PAI private key. DAC private key stored in secure element.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during factory provisioning (Unprovisioned -> Provisioned_Factory) to create device identity

functions.name: generate_random_key
functions.description: Generates cryptographically secure random key using hardware random number generator with sufficient entropy
functions.algorithm_detail: NIST SP 800-90B compliant DRBG (Deterministic Random Bit Generator) using hardware entropy source. Ensures unpredictability and adequate entropy for cryptographic keys.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during key generation (provisioning, commissioning, key rotation) to create keys with strong randomness

functions.name: apply_code_signature

parameters.name: code_signing_key
parameters.description: Private key for signing, stored in HSM

functions.description: Creates cryptographic signature over firmware image using manufacturer's code signing key stored in HSM
functions.algorithm_detail: ECDSA signature using P-256 curve over SHA-256 hash of firmware image. Code signing private key never leaves HSM. Signature appended to firmware image.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during factory provisioning to sign firmware, enabling verified boot

functions.name: configure_secure_boot

parameters.name: root_of_trust
parameters.description: Hardware-embedded public key for boot chain verification

functions.description: Configures device's immutable root of trust and enables verified boot chain validation
functions.algorithm_detail: Programs hardware with root public key hash. Each boot stage verifies signature of next stage using previous stage's embedded public key. Chain starts from immutable hardware root.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during factory fusing to enable secure boot (Unprovisioned -> Provisioned_Factory)

functions.name: fuse_debug_disable_bits
functions.description: Permanently disables debug interfaces (JTAG, SWD) by burning hardware fuses
functions.algorithm_detail: One-time programmable (OTP) fuse burning to disable debug ports. Irreversible operation ensuring debug interfaces cannot be re-enabled in production devices.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during factory fusing to protect against debug interface attacks (T117)

functions.name: store_in_secure_element
functions.description: Stores cryptographic key in hardware secure element (HSE/TPM/TEE) with protection against extraction
functions.algorithm_detail: Key stored in tamper-resistant hardware with access control. Key operations performed within secure boundary. Protection against side-channel attacks, memory dumps, physical probing.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called repeatedly to protect DAC keys, operational keys, and sensitive material

functions.name: generate_random_passcode
functions.description: Generates random commissioning passcode with specified entropy using secure random number generator
functions.algorithm_detail: NIST SP 800-90B compliant random generation. Minimum 27 bits entropy. Passcode format: 11-digit numeric or alphanumeric depending on commissioning method.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when device unpackaged or when administrator opens commissioning window with dynamic passcode

functions.name: derive_pbkdf_verifier
functions.description: Derives SPAKE2+ verifier from passcode using Password-Based Key Derivation Function with multiple iterations
functions.algorithm_detail: PBKDF2 or Argon2 with high iteration count. Output: SPAKE2+ verifier (w0, w1 values). Multiple hashes increase computational cost for offline attacks. CM99 requires multiple iterations.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called to create passcode verifier that can validate passcode without storing plaintext passcode

functions.name: clear_all_commissioning_state
functions.description: Deletes all temporary commissioning state information including rendezvous data
functions.algorithm_detail: Securely erases: discovery advertisement data, PASE session state, temporary keys, failed attempt counters. Ensures no residual information remains after commissioning window closes.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when commissioning window times out or commissioning fails (CM6 requirement)

functions.name: broadcast_discovery

parameters.name: version_info_level
parameters.description: Minimal, standard, or full - controls information disclosure

functions.description: Advertises device as commissionable via DNS-SD/mDNS with controlled information disclosure
functions.algorithm_detail: Broadcasts commissionable node discovery with minimal OS/version information (CM7). Does not advertise VID/PID before commissioning (CM183). Includes discriminator and pairing hint.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when commissioning window opens to advertise availability

functions.name: verify_firmware_cryptographic_identity
functions.description: Performs remote firmware attestation to cryptographically verify firmware identity and detect compromise
functions.algorithm_detail: Remote attestation protocol: device signs measurement of firmware with attestation key, verifier checks signature against trusted measurements. Detects unauthorized firmware modifications.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called at commissioning and revocation list updates (CM110) to verify firmware authenticity

functions.name: check_boot_chain_integrity
functions.description: Verifies secure boot chain integrity from immutable root of trust through all boot stages
functions.algorithm_detail: Validates cryptographic signatures of all boot stages: bootloader, kernel, firmware. Each stage verified by previous stage. Chain rooted in hardware immutable public key.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during attestation and boot to ensure firmware authenticity (CM22)

functions.name: activate_access_control_enforcement
functions.description: Activates Access Control List (ACL) enforcement for all resource access
functions.algorithm_detail: Enables ACL checking for all cluster operations. Every access request validated against ACL entries. Denies access if no matching ACL entry grants privilege. Enforces least privilege principle.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning to operational state to enforce access control (CM112)

functions.name: signal_error_or_recovery
functions.description: Enters recovery mode or signals error when secure boot fails or compromise detected
functions.algorithm_detail: Device resiliency feature (CM57): on secure boot failure, enters recovery mode (limited functionality, update only) or signals error via LED/status. Prevents silent compromise operation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when attestation fails or boot integrity check fails

functions.name: send_attack_alert
functions.description: Notifies administrator of detected attack or suspicious behavior
functions.algorithm_detail: Sends alert message to administrator nodes via secure channel describing attack type, affected resources, and recommended actions. Part of CM44 response mechanism.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when attack detected (excessive queries, malicious messages, suspicious behavior)

functions.name: activate_countermeasures
functions.description: Activates defensive measures in response to detected attack
functions.algorithm_detail: Enables: rate limiting, denylist enforcement, channel hopping, group key rotation, increased logging. Administrator-configurable response to attacks (CM44).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when transitioning to Under_Attack_Detected state

functions.name: enforce_query_rate_limits
functions.description: Applies rate limiting to resource-intensive operations, especially for battery-powered devices
functions.algorithm_detail: Token bucket or leaky bucket rate limiting. Battery devices can limit rate to zero if desired (CM51). Prevents DoS through excessive wakeups or queries.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in defensive mode or for battery devices under attack (T52, T53)

functions.name: add_malicious_node_to_denylist
functions.description: Adds node to denylist, blocking all communication from that node
functions.algorithm_detail: Maintains denylist of node IDs. All incoming messages from denylisted nodes dropped. Administrator action (CM44) to block malicious or compromised nodes.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when malicious node detected, part of attack response

functions.name: suspend_unsafe_operations
functions.description: Suspends all unsafe operations and reverts device to safe state
functions.algorithm_detail: For safety-critical devices: stops motors, locks doors in safe position, disables heating elements, etc. Prevents harm during outage or before validated operations (CM263).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called before unsafe operations or during outage (T231, T249)

functions.name: check_safety_limits
functions.description: Validates that requested settings are within safe operational limits
functions.algorithm_detail: Checks settings against manufacturer-defined safety bounds. Rejects settings that could cause harm (excessive temperature, voltage, speed, etc.). CM269 requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when settings changes requested, validates safety constraints

functions.name: download_firmware_update

parameters.name: ota_url
parameters.description: URL for firmware download from DCL or manufacturer

functions.description: Downloads OTA firmware update from authenticated source
functions.algorithm_detail: HTTPS download with TLS server authentication. Retrieves firmware image and detached signature. Source authenticated via DCL or direct manufacturer URL.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when firmware update available

functions.name: check_firmware_signature
functions.description: Verifies cryptographic signature of firmware update using code signing certificate
functions.algorithm_detail: ECDSA verification: hash firmware with SHA-256, verify signature using public key from code signing certificate. Certificate chain validated back to trusted root. CM58 requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called before installing OTA update to validate authenticity and integrity

functions.name: compare_version_numbers
functions.description: Compares version numbers to prevent rollback to older vulnerable firmware
functions.algorithm_detail: Semantic version comparison or monotonic counter check. Rejects updates to older versions to prevent rollback attacks (implied by T168 countermeasures).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during OTA update validation to prevent firmware downgrade

functions.name: install_verified_update
functions.description: Installs cryptographically verified firmware update
functions.algorithm_detail: Writes firmware to flash, updates boot configuration, increments anti-rollback counter. Secure boot will verify on next boot. Installation atomic if possible.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after firmware verification passes to install update

functions.name: create_firmware_backup
functions.description: Creates backup of current firmware before update installation
functions.algorithm_detail: Copies current firmware to backup partition or external storage. Enables rollback if new firmware fails secure boot or attestation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called before OTA installation for recovery capability

functions.name: remove_all_nocs
functions.description: Removes all Node Operational Certificates from all fabrics
functions.algorithm_detail: Deletes all NOC certificates, fabric information, and associated operational private keys. Part of factory reset (CM35).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during factory reset to remove commissioning data

functions.name: remove_encryption_keys
functions.description: Securely erases all encryption keys including operational keys and group keys
functions.algorithm_detail: Securely overwrites key material in memory and secure storage. Includes operational keys, group keys, session keys. Cryptographic erasure if supported. CM16 key rotation requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during factory reset and key rotation

functions.name: remove_all_access_controls
functions.description: Clears all ACL entries
functions.algorithm_detail: Removes all Access Control List entries. Device returns to default ACL state (typically administrator-only access).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during factory reset

functions.name: remove_user_application_data
functions.description: Removes all user data except data required to persist across resets
functions.algorithm_detail: Deletes user-created data, network credentials, scene configurations. Preserves factory-provisioned data (DAC, attestation key). CM35 factory reset requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during factory reset to prevent data leakage (T17, T82)

functions.name: generate_noc
functions.description: Generates Node Operational Certificate for device on specific fabric
functions.algorithm_detail: Commissioner generates NOC signed by fabric root CA. Contains device operational public key, fabric ID, node ID. Private key generated and protected by device.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during successful commissioning to create operational credentials

functions.name: apply_default_acls
functions.description: Installs default Access Control List granting administrator full access
functions.algorithm_detail: Creates default ACL entry: administrator node has Administer privilege for all clusters on all endpoints. Starting point for ACL configuration.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during commissioning to establish initial access control

functions.name: generate_new_session_keys

parameters.name: use_pfs
parameters.description: Whether to use perfect forward secrecy

functions.description: Generates new session keys with perfect forward secrecy properties
functions.algorithm_detail: ECDHE (Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman Ephemeral) key agreement. Session keys not compromised even if long-term keys compromised. CM17 requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during commissioning and key rotation to establish PFS

functions.name: delete_operational_certificate
functions.description: Removes operational certificate for revoked node
functions.algorithm_detail: Deletes NOC and associated credentials for specified node ID. Part of revocation process (CM259). Node loses network access.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when operational certificate revoked

functions.name: remove_access_for_revoked_node
functions.description: Updates ACLs to remove all access for revoked node
functions.algorithm_detail: Removes or updates ACL entries mentioning revoked node. Ensures revoked node has no ACL-granted access. CM20 revocation requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during credential revocation

functions.name: enforce_revocation

parameters.name: revoked_node_id
parameters.description: Node ID that has been revoked

functions.description: Enforces revocation by rejecting all communication from revoked node
functions.algorithm_detail: Adds revoked node to rejection list. All messages from revoked node dropped. Secure channels with revoked node terminated.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after credential revocation to enforce denial of access

functions.name: enumerate_all_fabrics
functions.description: Returns list of all fabrics device is commissioned on
functions.algorithm_detail: Queries Node Operational Credentials cluster, returns fabric index, compressed fabric ID, NOC info for each fabric. CM41 visibility requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when administrator views fabrics (T173-T175 detection)

functions.name: negotiate_secure_channel_config
functions.description: Negotiates secure channel parameters with peer
functions.algorithm_detail: CASE protocol negotiation: cipher suite, protocol version, session parameters. Both parties must agree on configuration. CM45 requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during secure channel establishment

functions.name: validate_secure_channel_config
functions.description: Validates negotiated secure channel configuration meets security requirements
functions.algorithm_detail: Checks: cipher suite strength, protocol version supported, no known weak configurations. Rejects weak or misconfigured channels. CM45 requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after negotiation to validate configuration before establishing channel

functions.name: create_encrypted_session
functions.description: Establishes encrypted secure session with validated parameters
functions.algorithm_detail: Creates AES-CCM encrypted session using negotiated keys. All subsequent messages encrypted and authenticated. Protects confidentiality and integrity.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after successful negotiation and validation to establish secure channel

functions.name: check_length_type_range
functions.description: Validates input for length, character type, and acceptable values/ranges
functions.algorithm_detail: Performs input validation as specified in CM46: string length limits, numeric ranges, character set validation, ACL entry validation, enum value checking. Prevents injection and overflow attacks.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called before applying any configuration or input changes

functions.name: apply_if_valid
functions.description: Applies validated input to device configuration
functions.algorithm_detail: Only executes if validation passed. Safely applies configuration changes, ACL updates, or command parameters.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after successful input validation

functions.name: reject_if_invalid
functions.description: Rejects invalid input and returns error response
functions.algorithm_detail: Returns error status code indicating validation failure. Does not apply input. Prevents exploitation through malformed inputs (T55, T59, T185).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when input validation fails

functions.name: apply_minimal_necessary_privileges
functions.description: Grants bridge only minimal privileges necessary for bridged device functionality
functions.algorithm_detail: Principle of least privilege for bridges (CM149). Only grants access to clusters and endpoints necessary for bridged devices. Prevents privilege escalation (T162, T165, T167).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when bridge is activated to limit attack surface

functions.name: verify_all_bridged_device_identity
functions.description: Authenticates and verifies identity of all bridged devices
functions.algorithm_detail: Bridge authenticates each bridged device using bridged ecosystem's authentication mechanism. Verifies device identity before exposing to Matter network. CM149 requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during bridge activation to ensure bridged device authenticity

functions.name: package_with_rf_protection
functions.description: Applies RF shielding to packaging to prevent NFC eavesdropping
functions.algorithm_detail: Metallized packaging or RF-blocking material prevents passive NFC tag reading through package. Protects against T131 attacks. CM135 requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during device packaging

functions.name: apply_removable_flap
functions.description: Applies removable flap or cover to conceal QR code when not in use
functions.algorithm_detail: Physical removable flap covers QR code. User removes when commissioning. Prevents long-range camera capture (T90). CM89 requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during packaging/manufacturing

functions.name: orient_nfc_tag_directionally
functions.description: Positions NFC tag to be readable only from specific direction
functions.algorithm_detail: NFC antenna oriented and shielded so readable only from intended surface. Reduces eavesdropping risk. CM136 requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during device assembly/packaging

functions.name: create_random_passcode
functions.description: Creates random passcode meeting entropy requirements
functions.algorithm_detail: Cryptographically secure random generation with specified entropy. For dynamic passcodes (CM252) or initial provisioning. Minimum 27 bits (CM5).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when creating dynamic passcodes or during factory reset

functions.name: verify_ca_trust_worthiness
functions.description: Validates that root CA certificate is trustworthy before adding to trust store
functions.algorithm_detail: Commissioner/Administrator performs due diligence on CA certificate: validates certificate chain, checks revocation status, verifies CA policy compliance, ensures CA is from known trustworthy source. CM36 requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when administrator adds root CA to prevent T153 attacks

functions.name: install_trusted_root_ca
functions.description: Installs validated root CA certificate into device trust store
functions.algorithm_detail: Adds CA certificate to root trust store. All future certificate validations will trust certificates signed by this CA. Only performed after CA trustworthiness verified.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after CA validation to add to trust store

functions.name: validate_content_source_identity
functions.description: Validates and verifies identity and origin of content sharing source
functions.algorithm_detail: Cryptographically verifies content source identity. Validates commissioner identity for content sharing. Checks ACL permissions. CM248 requirement for content launching features.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called before allowing content sharing to prevent phishing (T240)

functions.name: show_human_friendly_origin_display
functions.description: Displays human-friendly information about content source origin before sharing
functions.algorithm_detail: Shows user clear identification of where content is coming from. Includes application name, device name, user identifier. CM248 requirement to prevent phishing.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during content sharing setup to inform user of content source

functions.name: apply_content_rating_restrictions
functions.description: Enforces parental control restrictions on content based on ratings
functions.algorithm_detail: Checks content rating against configured parental control settings. Blocks content exceeding allowed rating. Informs user of limitations (e.g., when settings don't apply to all content sources). CM251 requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during content sharing to enforce parental controls (T243)

functions.name: configure_webrtc_secure_channel
functions.description: Configures WebRTC with minimum required security for content sharing
functions.algorithm_detail: Implements w3c and webrtc-security requirements: DTLS for media encryption, SRTP for real-time transport security, proper certificate validation. CM249 requirement to prevent MITM (T241).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when establishing WebRTC session for content sharing

functions.name: assign_unique_acl_for_client
functions.description: Creates unique ACL entry for content sharing client for independent access control and revocation
functions.algorithm_detail: Creates specific ACL entry for content sharing client separate from primary device admin account. Enables granular access control management and revocation without impacting admin. CM250 requirement for content clients (T242, T244).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when content sharing client connects to enable independent revocation

functions.name: remove_client_specific_acl
functions.description: Removes ACL entry specific to content sharing client
functions.algorithm_detail: Deletes ACL entry for content client, revoking access. Does not impact primary admin account access. CM250 requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when content sharing session ends or suspicious activity detected

functions.name: detect_and_surface_suspicious_behavior
functions.description: Detects suspicious behavior from unauthenticated network clients and notifies user with option to disable
functions.algorithm_detail: Monitors unauthenticated client behavior: excessive UDC messages, unusual patterns, DoS attempts. Surfaces suspicious activity to user with option to disable functionality exposed to unauthenticated clients. CM253 requirement (T244, T246).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called continuously for unauthenticated client monitoring

functions.name: mark_data_untrusted
functions.description: Marks data from unauthenticated sources as advisory only, not for automatic actions
functions.algorithm_detail: Tags data with untrusted flag. Prevents data from triggering automated actions (e.g., energy management decisions, door unlocking). CM254 requirement to prevent power grid attacks (T239) and other unauthenticated data exploits.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when receiving data from unauthenticated sources

functions.name: prevent_automatic_actions
functions.description: Prevents unauthenticated data from triggering automatic device actions
functions.algorithm_detail: Blocks automatic responses to unauthenticated data. Requires user confirmation for any actions based on untrusted data. Prevents energy management manipulation (T239).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called to prevent automatic actions on untrusted data

functions.name: alert_user_device_should_appear_on_network
functions.description: Notifies user that device should appear on operational network after NFC phase 1, enabling detection of NFC recommissioning attacks
functions.algorithm_detail: After NFC commissioning phase 1 completes, commissioner alerts user device should appear on network in phase 2. User can detect if device doesn't appear (indicating attacker intercepted). CM276 requirement to prevent T255.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after NFC phase 1 to enable user detection of attacks

functions.name: detect_dcl_changes_relating_to_vendor
functions.description: Monitors DCL for changes relating to vendor's products and credentials
functions.algorithm_detail: DCL writers continuously monitor blockchain for changes to their PAAs, PAIs, DACs, OTA URLs, revocations. Detects unauthorized modifications. CM76 requirement to detect DCL compromise (T168, T233, T234).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called continuously for DCL monitoring

functions.name: verify_all_dcl_writes_authorized
functions.description: Validates that all DCL write operations are authorized by proper entities
functions.algorithm_detail: Light clients cryptographically verify responses from DCL nodes (CM199). Validates signatures, checks authorization of writers. Detects malicious proposals and revocations.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for DCL write verification

functions.name: alert_if_changes_unauthorized
functions.description: Raises alert if unauthorized DCL changes detected
functions.algorithm_detail: If monitoring detects unauthorized DCL changes (wrong OTA URL, illegitimate revocation, etc.), raises immediate alert to vendor security team. CM76 requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when unauthorized DCL activity detected

functions.name: alert_user_of_suspicious_activity
functions.description: Notifies user of detected suspicious behavior with relevant details
functions.algorithm_detail: Displays user notification with details of suspicious activity: source, type of behavior, recommended actions. CM253 requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when suspicious behavior detected from any source

definitions.definition: X.509 certificate containing device-unique public key, Vendor ID, Product ID, signed by Product Attestation Intermediate (PAI). Proves device authenticity and manufacturer identity.
definitions.security_relevance: Foundation of device identity and trust. Compromise enables cloning (T22, T34, T86). Must be unique per device (CM23) and private key protected (CM77).

definitions.definition: X.509 certificate issued by fabric administrator containing device operational public key and fabric-specific node ID. Grants device membership in fabric.
definitions.security_relevance: Enables device operation on fabric. Compromise allows impersonation (T87, T110). Must be revocable (CM259). Private key must be protected (CM87).

definitions.definition: Commissioning protocol using SPAKE2+ for password-authenticated key exchange without revealing passcode to eavesdroppers.
definitions.security_relevance: Vulnerable to online brute force if rate limiting absent (T101, T112). Requires 27-bit entropy passcode (CM5) and attempt limiting (CM100). Offline attacks possible if verifier leaked (T102).

definitions.definition: Protocol for establishing secure sessions between commissioned nodes using operational certificates for mutual authentication.
definitions.security_relevance: Protects operational communication after commissioning. Requires secure channel negotiation and validation (CM45). Vulnerable if certificates compromised or improperly validated.

definitions.definition: Function that derives cryptographic keys from passwords using iterative hashing to increase computational cost of brute force attacks.
definitions.security_relevance: Used to derive passcode verifier for SPAKE2+. Multiple iterations required (CM99) to resist offline brute force (T102). Insufficient iterations weaken passcode protection.

definitions.definition: Property that compromise of long-term keys does not compromise past session keys. Achieved through ephemeral key exchange (ECDHE).
definitions.security_relevance: Required by CM17. Protects past communications if long-term keys compromised (T17, T94). Critical for limiting damage from key extraction.

definitions.definition: Boot process where each stage cryptographically verifies the next stage before execution, rooted in hardware immutable trust anchor.
definitions.security_relevance: Required by CM22. Prevents execution of malicious firmware (T20). Immutable root of trust prevents compromise of boot chain. Failures must trigger recovery (CM57).

definitions.definition: Remote verification of device firmware identity using cryptographic measurements signed by attestation key.
definitions.security_relevance: Required by CM110 at commissioning and revocation updates. Detects compromised firmware (T9, T226). Without attestation, malicious firmware operates undetected.

definitions.definition: List of entries defining which nodes have which privileges (Administer, Manage, Operate, View) for which clusters/endpoints.
definitions.security_relevance: Foundation of authorization (CM112). Improper ACLs enable unauthorized access (T9, T120, T230). Administrator must carefully control privileges especially for safety-critical systems (CM244).

definitions.definition: Time-limited state where device accepts commissioning connections. Opened by physical action or administrator.
definitions.security_relevance: Attack window for unauthorized commissioning (T15, T101, T173-175). Must timeout (CM8: 15 min max), require physical action for initial commissioning (CM2, CM3), and have attempt limits (CM100).

definitions.definition: Process returning device to factory state by removing all commissioning data, keys, credentials, ACLs, and user data.
definitions.security_relevance: Required for secure device transfer/disposal (CM15, CM35). Must remove all keys (CM16), apply key rotation (CM16), prevent data leakage (T17, T82). Must have physical button (CM15).

definitions.definition: Independent administrative domain with its own root CA, operational certificates, and access control. Device can be commissioned on multiple fabrics.
definitions.security_relevance: Fabric isolation critical (implied requirement). Compromise of one fabric must not affect others. Administrator must view all fabrics (CM41) to detect unauthorized additions (T173-T175).

definitions.definition: Device that exposes non-Matter devices (bridged devices) to Matter network by translating between protocols.
definitions.security_relevance: Security impedance mismatch risk (T162, T165, T167). Bridge security often weaker link. Administrator must only grant privileges comfortable granting to ALL bridged devices (CM149). Authentication of bridged devices required.

definitions.definition: Blockchain ledger storing device attestation certificates, revocations, and OTA URLs. Distributed across validator and observer nodes.
definitions.security_relevance: Critical infrastructure. Compromise enables malicious OTA URLs (T168), illegitimate revocations (T233, T234), DoS (T169, T180, T182, T185). Requires private key HSM protection (CM167), access restrictions (CM163), monitoring (CM76).

definitions.definition: Mechanism to limit frequency of operations, especially for battery devices and resource-intensive operations.
definitions.security_relevance: Defense against DoS attacks (T52, T53, T246). Battery devices must implement (CM51), can reduce rate to zero. Prevents excessive wakeups and battery drain attacks.

definitions.definition: SPAKE2+ verifier (w0, w1 values) derived from setup passcode via PBKDF, stored on device instead of plaintext passcode.
definitions.security_relevance: Enables passcode verification without storing passcode. Vulnerable to offline brute force if leaked (T102). Multiple PBKDF iterations required (CM99) to increase cracking cost.

definitions.definition: Randomly generated passcode created when commissioning window opened, rather than static printed passcode.
definitions.security_relevance: Mitigates static passcode vulnerabilities (T245). Required for screen devices (CM252). Prevents brute forcing static passcode over time. Static passcodes must be secured after commissioning (CM152).

definitions.definition: Devices affecting physical security such as door locks, cameras, barriers, closures.
definitions.security_relevance: Enhanced protection requirements (T260). QR code and power on secure side (CM280), user warning about vulnerability window (CM282), physical interaction for commissioning must be protected (CM4). Higher risk of physical access consequences.

definitions.definition: Intermediate CA certificate used by manufacturer to sign DACs. Issued by Product Attestation Authority (PAA) in DCL.
definitions.security_relevance: Trust anchor for device attestation chain. Must be designed to prevent unauthorized device creation (CM117). Compromise enables counterfeit devices with proper credentials (T23, T232).

definitions.definition: Private key used by manufacturer to sign firmware images. Public key embedded in device for signature verification.
definitions.security_relevance: Critical key (T72). Compromise enables malicious firmware on all devices. Must be HSM-protected (CM28) with restricted access. Enables secure boot and verified updates (CM21, CM58).

definitions.definition: Feature enabling devices to share video/media content with origin verification, parental controls, and WebRTC security.
definitions.security_relevance: Attack vector for phishing (T240), MITM (T241), traceability issues (T242), parental control bypass (T243). Requires content origin verification (CM248), WebRTC security (CM249), unique ACLs per client (CM250), parental control enforcement (CM251).

definitions.definition: Random value used with cryptographic algorithms to ensure same plaintext encrypts to different ciphertext each time.
definitions.security_relevance: Predictable IVs enable key derivation attacks (T81). Must be cryptographically random (CM78). IV reuse with same key catastrophically breaks encryption security.

definitions.definition: Hardware and physical security measures protecting devices against tampering, probing, and side-channel attacks.
definitions.security_relevance: Required for some devices (CM62, CM139). Prevents physical tampering (T60), key extraction via hardware attacks, debug interface exploitation (T117). Protection level varies by device type and threat model.

definitions.definition: Near-field communication protocol for commissioning using passive NFC tags with onboarding payload.
definitions.security_relevance: Vulnerable to eavesdropping (T131) and recommissioning attacks (T255). Requires RF shielding in packaging (CM135), directional positioning (CM136), RSSI detection (CM133), user notification after phase 1 (CM276). Readable up to 40cm with specialized equipment.

definitions.definition: Data received from sources that have not been authenticated or verified by cryptographic means.
definitions.security_relevance: Must be treated as advisory only (CM254). Cannot trigger automatic actions to prevent power grid manipulation (T239), DoS (T246). Suspicious behavior must be surfaced to user (CM253).

definitions.definition: Blockchain-based distributed ledger storing device attestation certificates, revocations, OTA URLs, and compliance information.
definitions.security_relevance: Critical infrastructure vulnerable to: key exfiltration (T168), validator DoS (T169, T177, T180), observer DoS (T182), malicious writes (T185), illegitimate revocations (T233, T234). Requires HSM key protection (CM167), access restrictions (CM163), continuous monitoring (CM76, CM199), input validation (CM169).

definitions.definition: Mechanisms to restrict content access based on rating and age-appropriateness per regional regulations.
definitions.security_relevance: Bypass enables children to access inappropriate content (T243). Must be enforced for all content sources (CM251) with user notification of limitations. Regulatory compliance requirement.

definitions.definition: Security requirements for Web Real-Time Communication including DTLS, SRTP, and certificate validation.
definitions.security_relevance: Without proper implementation, vulnerable to MITM and eavesdropping (T241). Must implement w3c and webrtc-security.github.io requirements (CM249). Critical for content sharing features.

definitions.definition: Attacks that extract cryptographic keys remotely via software vulnerabilities, side channels, or timing attacks.
definitions.security_relevance: Enables attacker to derive keys without physical access (T94). Requires protection against known remote extraction techniques (CM107). Compromised keys enable control of multiple devices if keys reused.

security_properties.description: Initial commissioning must be initiated via physical user interaction (button press, power cycle, NFC tap), preventing remote commissioning of uncommissioned devices
Subsequent commissioning of already commissioned device requires administrator authentication and consent, preventing silent re-commissioning by unauthorized parties
Commissioning window automatically closes after maximum 15 minutes (900 seconds) if commissioning not completed, limiting attack window
Setup passcode must have minimum 27 bits of entropy from cryptographically secure random generation, resistant to online and offline brute force
Device exits commissioning mode after 20 failed commissioning attempts, preventing online brute force attacks
All firmware must be cryptographically signed by manufacturer and verified before execution via secure boot from immutable root of trust
Each device has unique Device Attestation Certificate and private key, preventing device cloning
All cryptographic private keys (DAC, operational, session) stored and protected in secure element/HSM preventing extraction
critical_transitions: Any transition generating or storing keys (store_in_secure_element)

security_properties.description: Factory reset must remove all commissioning data, operational credentials, keys, ACLs, and user data except explicitly persistent data, with key rotation
Session keys derived using ephemeral key exchange ensuring past session keys remain secure even if long-term keys compromised
Device firmware identity cryptographically verified at commissioning and revocation list updates via remote attestation with trusted root of measurement
All resource access must be validated against Access Control Lists, enforcing principle of least privilege
System detects suspicious behavior and attacks, notifies administrator, and activates defensive countermeasures (rate limiting, denylisting, key rotation)
Safety-critical devices must revert to safe state before performing unsafe operations and during outages, with setting validation against safety limits
OTA firmware updates must be cryptographically signed and verified, with rollback prevention to older vulnerable versions
All configuration inputs and commands must be validated for length, type, range, and acceptable values before application
Bridges granted only minimal necessary privileges, with authentication of all bridged devices, as administrator is comfortable granting to all bridged devices
critical_transitions: Operational_Verified -> Bridge_Connected (bridge_activation with privilege limitation and authentication)

security_properties.description: Physical security devices (locks, cameras, barriers) must have commissioning interfaces and QR codes on secure side with user warnings about vulnerability windows
System must support operational certificate revocation with immediate enforcement, removing access for revoked nodes
All temporary commissioning state and rendezvous information must be deleted upon timeout or failure
Secure channel configuration must be negotiated and validated by both parties before establishment
Resource-intensive operations must have rate limiting, especially for battery devices which can limit to zero
Commissioning must complete atomically with all credentials, ACLs, and certificates installed, or fail completely with state cleanup
Administrators must be able to view all fabrics device is commissioned on to detect unauthorized fabric additions
Screen devices must use dynamic randomly-generated passcodes rather than static passcodes to prevent brute force over time
System must detect compromise via attestation failures and enter recovery mode or signal error, preventing silent compromise operation
Commissioners and administrators only add root CA certificates that are verified as trustworthy, preventing MITM via untrusted CAs
Content sharing features must verify and display content source origin with user confirmation before sharing
Devices must enforce parental control restrictions on content based on ratings and inform users of limitations
WebRTC communications must implement minimum security requirements including DTLS, SRTP, and proper certificate validation
Content sharing clients must have unique ACL entries separate from admin accounts enabling independent revocation
Data from unauthenticated sources must be treated as advisory only and not trigger automatic actions
System must detect and surface suspicious behavior from unauthenticated clients with user option to disable exposure
After NFC commissioning phase 1, user must be notified device should appear on network to detect recommissioning attacks
Devices must implement physical protection against tampering and key extraction appropriate to their threat model
critical_transitions: Any state -> Compromised_Silent (physical_tampering_successful if protections inadequate)

security_properties.description: All cryptographic operations must use cryptographically random initialization vectors, never predictable or reused
DCL writers must continuously monitor for unauthorized changes and raise alerts for illegitimate modifications
Devices must implement protection against known remote attacks that can extract or infer cryptographic keys

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: ECDSA P-256 key pair generation, X.509 certificate signing
cryptographic_operations.output: X.509 DAC certificate and ECDSA P-256 private key
assumptions: Device private key stored in secure element

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: ECDSA P-256 signature over SHA-256 hash
assumptions: Code signing key never leaves HSM

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: ECDSA P-256 signature verification chain from immutable root
PBKDF2 or Argon2 with high iteration count

cryptographic_operations.output: Shared session key independent of long-term keys
assumptions: Ephemeral keys securely erased after use
Nonce never reused with same key

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: ECDSA P-256 signature verification over SHA-256
Device signs firmware measurements with DAC key
assumptions: Measurements taken at boot by trusted code

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: X.509 certificate generation with ECDSA P-256
NIST SP 800-90B DRBG from hardware entropy

security_assumptions.assumption: Factory security controls in place (secure fusing station, HSM for code signing, physical security)
security_assumptions.if_violated: Factory compromise enables malicious firmware, cloned devices, extracted keys (T20, T22, T72, T117)

security_assumptions.assumption: Hardware secure element/HSM properly configured and maintains security properties
security_assumptions.if_violated: Private keys (DAC, operational, session) extractable via memory dump or side channels (T17, T22, T24, T94)

security_assumptions.assumption: Cryptographic algorithms (ECDSA P-256, AES-128-CCM, SHA-256, SPAKE2+) remain secure
security_assumptions.if_violated: All cryptographic protections fail; encryption broken, signatures forged, keys extracted

security_assumptions.assumption: Random number generators provide adequate entropy per NIST SP 800-90B
security_assumptions.if_violated: Predictable keys and passcodes enable brute force attacks (T39, T81, T101, T102)

security_assumptions.assumption: Immutable hardware root of trust cannot be modified or bypassed
security_assumptions.if_violated: Secure boot compromised, malicious firmware executes undetected (T9, T20, T59)

security_assumptions.assumption: Administrator/Commissioner nodes are trustworthy and not compromised
security_assumptions.if_violated: Malicious administrator can add untrustworthy root CAs (T153), grant excessive privileges, extract sensitive data (T120)

security_assumptions.assumption: Physical access to device can be controlled or limited for tamper-sensitive devices
security_assumptions.if_violated: Physical tampering enables QR code capture, device reset, debug interface access (T3, T60, T84, T90, T260)

security_assumptions.assumption: Users perform factory reset before selling or discarding devices
security_assumptions.if_violated: Previous owner's keys, credentials, and data recoverable by new owner (T17, T82)

security_assumptions.assumption: PKI Certificate Authorities (PAA, PAI) follow certificate policies and are not compromised
security_assumptions.if_violated: Counterfeit devices with valid credentials, unauthorized certificate issuance (T23, T232)

security_assumptions.assumption: DCL infrastructure remains sufficiently distributed and validator nodes independent
security_assumptions.if_violated: DCL compromise or DoS affects certificate revocation, OTA updates (T168, T169, T177, T180, T182)

security_assumptions.assumption: Network layer (Wi-Fi, Thread, Ethernet) provides baseline transport integrity
security_assumptions.if_violated: Layer 2 attacks could compromise secure channel establishment or enable traffic injection

security_assumptions.assumption: Implementation follows specification correctly without implementation vulnerabilities
security_assumptions.if_violated: Buffer overflows, logic errors, race conditions enable exploitation (T9, T59)

security_assumptions.assumption: Secure boot chain extends from immutable root through all firmware layers without gaps
security_assumptions.if_violated: Malicious code can execute before verification stage, compromising boot process

security_assumptions.assumption: Cryptographic operations implemented without side-channel vulnerabilities (timing, power, EM)
security_assumptions.if_violated: Keys extractable via side-channel analysis even with secure element (T94)

security_assumptions.assumption: ACL evaluation logic implemented correctly without bypass vulnerabilities
security_assumptions.if_violated: Access control ineffective, unauthorized nodes gain access (T9, T120)

security_assumptions.assumption: OTA update channel secure and authenticated (DCL or direct manufacturer)
security_assumptions.if_violated: Malicious firmware updates possible even with signature checking (T168)

security_assumptions.assumption: Device has sufficient secure storage for keys, credentials, ACLs, fabric data
security_assumptions.if_violated: Cannot store required security material, may use insecure storage

security_assumptions.assumption: Operating system and firmware execution environment not compromised by exploitation
security_assumptions.if_violated: All application-level security bypassed if OS compromised (T9, T16, T59)

security_assumptions.assumption: Bridge implementations correctly map Matter security to bridged ecosystem security
security_assumptions.if_violated: Security impedance mismatch enables privilege escalation and compromise (T162, T165, T167)

security_assumptions.assumption: Users recognize and respond to security warnings and notifications
security_assumptions.if_violated: Warning systems ineffective, silent attacks succeed despite detection (T173-175, T260)

security_assumptions.assumption: Commissioning happens in window where attacker cannot physically intercept or MITM
security_assumptions.if_violated: MITM attacks during commissioning possible if extended window or attacker has prolonged physical presence

security_assumptions.assumption: PBKDF iteration count sufficiently high to resist offline brute force with current computing power
security_assumptions.if_violated: Passcode verifier crackable in reasonable time if leaked (T102)

security_assumptions.assumption: Manufacturer monitors for vulnerabilities and provides timely updates
security_assumptions.if_violated: Known vulnerabilities remain unpatched, exploitation continues (T9)

security_assumptions.assumption: Factory reset mechanism itself cannot be compromised or bypassed by malware
security_assumptions.if_violated: Persistent malware survives factory reset (T16)

security_assumptions.assumption: Device has accurate time source for certificate validity checking
security_assumptions.if_violated: Cannot properly validate certificate expiration, may accept expired certificates or reject valid ones

security_assumptions.assumption: Initialization vectors generated with cryptographic randomness, never reused with same key
security_assumptions.if_violated: Predictable IVs enable key derivation attacks, IV reuse catastrophically breaks encryption (T81)

security_assumptions.assumption: Physical protection mechanisms appropriate to device threat model implemented
security_assumptions.if_violated: Physical tampering enables key extraction, device modification, unauthorized access (T60)

security_assumptions.assumption: Administrators exercise due diligence before adding root CA certificates to trust store
security_assumptions.if_violated: Untrusted CAs enable MITM attacks, NOC impersonation, secure channel compromise (T153)

security_assumptions.assumption: Content sharing features verify origin and enforce parental controls correctly
security_assumptions.if_violated: Phishing attacks (T240), inappropriate content access (T243), user confusion about content source (T242)

security_assumptions.assumption: WebRTC implementation follows w3c and webrtc-security.github.io security requirements
security_assumptions.if_violated: Content sharing vulnerable to MITM and eavesdropping (T241)

security_assumptions.assumption: DCL infrastructure remains sufficiently distributed with independent validator nodes
security_assumptions.if_violated: DCL vulnerable to consensus attacks, DoS of validators affects availability (T169, T177, T180)

security_assumptions.assumption: DCL writers continuously monitor for unauthorized changes and respond promptly
security_assumptions.if_violated: Malicious DCL changes (OTA URLs, revocations) go undetected (T168, T233, T234)

security_assumptions.assumption: Devices implement protection against known remote key extraction techniques
security_assumptions.if_violated: Keys extractable remotely via side channels, timing attacks, or software vulnerabilities (T94)

security_assumptions.assumption: Unauthenticated data treated as advisory only, never triggers automatic high-impact actions
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attackers can manipulate energy management, trigger unsafe operations via unauthenticated data (T239)

security_assumptions.assumption: NFC commissioning users notified when device should appear on network after phase 1
security_assumptions.if_violated: NFC recommissioning attacks go undetected (T255)

security_assumptions.assumption: Physical security devices (locks, barriers) have QR codes and power on secure side
security_assumptions.if_violated: Distraction attacks during installation enable credential theft and unauthorized commissioning (T260)
